title: New England Clam Chowder for Two
description: A creamy New England clam chowder with bacon, Yukon Gold potatoes, and chopped clams, thickened by pureeing some of the solids. Finished with parsley and lemon.

items: 2 (6.5 ounce) cans chopped clams (save the juice)

instructions: In a large pot, cook the diced bacon over medium heat until crispy.
Add the butter, onions, and celery. Season with salt and pepper and cook until the onions are translucent and the celery has softened, about 5–6 minutes.
Add the potatoes, season with salt, and cook for another 3–4 minutes. Add the garlic and cook for another 1–2 minutes.
Add the white wine, clam juice, heavy cream, Worcestershire, hot sauce, and bay leaves. Stir to combine and simmer for at least 20 minutes.
Add the clams with their juice.
With a slotted spoon, scoop out some of the solids and puree in a blender, then return the puree to the pot to thicken the chowder.
Add the parsley and season with salt and pepper if needed. Garnish with lemon wedges. Enjoy!
